_comment: Curated seed for a BLOCKED / WHAT-TO-TRY-NEXT section, same pattern as occupations.json.
WHY THIS EXISTS: TRACKER.md counts what is missing. It cannot tell you whether a gap is a
five-minute lookup or a person who has vanished. Twice now a 'one missing census' has eaten an
hour and ended in nothing attached. This file carries what was ALREADY TRIED and what to try
NEXT, so no one re-runs a failed search or re-derives a dead end.
RULE: 'tried' is a record of searches that FAILED. Nothing here is evidence and nothing here
may be attached to the tree. If a lead comes good it moves to the journal and the tree, and
Each entry ticks itself off when the named gap closes in an export - same mechanism as §7.

blocked.name: John Farrar
tried: 1851 name=John Farrar + birth=1805 +/-5 and +/-10 - 10 hits, none in the Bradford area, none with a wife Hannah. The filter was the problem: his birth year 1805 is ITSELF unsourced, so I was filtering real records out using an invented number
1851 name=Jane Farrar + birth=1832 Wibsey - 7 hits, no Wibsey Jane (and 1832 is unsourced too; the register says she was 31 in May 1868, so abt 1837)
1851 name=Hannah Farrar + birth=1803 Low Moor - ONE hit, a Newcastle woman. Wrong on every axis

1851 name=John Farrar + residence=Bradford, NO birth year - this finally bound properly and returned 50 real Bradford-area hits, 20 of them heads of household. Only one had a wife Hannah: John Farrar 38, b. Heptonstall, Halifax St James - opened it, and the household is a SCHOOL (ten boarders aged 7-12 plus a servant). Not him
blocked.found_instead: The 1868 marriage register (coll 2253 rec 4810060, Bradford St Peter, entry 286) READ FROM THE IMAGE proves the man himself: Jane Farrar, 31, spinster, weaver, of Bradford - father JOHN FARRAR, BLACKSMITH. The index does not carry the bride's father at all; only the image does. So 'blacksmith' is real and record-proven, but it is the ONLY thing about him that is.
next: DONE - the 1851 image was read and the household IS Farrar. Attached to Jane AND to Hannah, so Hannah went from ZERO sources to one. The image corrected two of my index-based guesses: Hannah is 'Mar' (MARRIED, husband absent) NOT a widow, and 'Wettey M Bradford' is 'WIBSEY nr BRADFORD'

CHRIS TO DECIDE - Hannah's birthplace: the tree says 1803 Low Moor (unsourced, origin unknown); the 1851 census image says abt 1804, Wibsey nr Bradford. I did NOT overwrite it and did NOT let the census cite the Low Moor value, because changing a direct ancestor's birthplace is your call

TRIED AND FAILED - hunting John in the 1851 census ELSEWHERE (Hannah is 'Mar' with husband absent, so he is enumerated somewhere else that night). Searched coll 8860 name=John Farrar + keyword=blacksmith: the keyword param does NOT bind, returned the same generic 50, and there is no John Farrar lodging or heading a household anywhere in the Bradford/Bowling area. Halifax has several but none tie to Hannah

NEXT ON JOHN - try the trade from the other end: UK City and County Directories (coll 3145) for a FARRAR, blacksmith, Bradford/Bowling/Wibsey 1840s-60s. A blacksmith is a rated, listed tradesman. Also worth a Bradford burial search for John Farrar 1851-1868 in case he died before the daughter's wedding (the register names a father whether alive or dead)
NEXT - read Rachel's surname off the image at higher magnification (cropped the canvas but not enough to be certain). Ancestry read 'Rachel Segltowler'; it is probably LIGHTOWLER

LEAD ONLY, NOT EVIDENCE - Hannah's maiden name may be LIGHTOWLERS. Her profile carries unattached hints for parents Daniel Lightowlers + Mary Bentley; the 24yo daughter in the 1851 house is transcribed 'Segltowler'; and Hannah's son is named Daniel. TESTED AND FAILED: searched coll 2253 for Hannah Lightowler - 48 hits across Bradford/Halifax/Leeds, NOT ONE marries a Farrar. Do not attach

WARNING - John Farrar's birth 1805 Halifax is still unsourced (his only record, the 1868 marriage, proves name + trade + fatherhood, nothing else). His citation has been re-pointed off the Birth fact onto Occupation 1868 so the tree no longer overstates it
Search 1851/1861 Bradford on the TRADE: a blacksmith is a rated, named tradesman - try UK City and County Directories (coll 3145) for a Farrar, blacksmith, Bradford, 1840s-60s

Work backwards from Jane instead: she is in the 1871/1881/1891 censuses already attached. Find her in 1851 or 1861 as a daughter at home and the whole household - father, mother, siblings - comes with her in one record

DONE - the 1868 register IS now attached to him (zero sources -> one). Method: open the merge URL, UNTICK EVERY checkbox (the ticked ones were 'Marriage to Jane Farrar' and 'Include Jane Farrar' as a NEW PERSON duplicate), then Save to tree. That attaches the citation alone, changing no facts and creating no people. Verified after reload: relationships still Hannah + Jane only

STILL TO FIX - Ancestry hung that citation on his BIRTH fact, implying the register evidences 1805 Halifax. It does not; it evidences his NAME, his TRADE and that he fathered Jane. Re-point it to the Occupation 1868 fact (needs the edit-citation dialog, so needs screenshots working)

blocked.name: Alfred Whitehead
tried: 65 Alfred Whiteheads b.1884-88 in coll 63150 - none in the right place
spouse=Alice and spouse=Emily - nothing (Emily Mallinson is NOT his wife; that was a misread of the next GEDCOM record)
residence=Leeds exact - returned zero, filter too tight

birthplace=Canada, which should have been decisive since he was born 27 Dec 1886 at York Mills, Toronto - EXACTLY ONE hit in the whole collection and it is a different man (Alfred B Whitehead b.1866, visitor in Westminster)
blocked.found_instead: Alice's 1921 (rec 36648706): she and baby Annie were at her MOTHER's house, 15 Willington Lane, Leeds. Alfred is not in the household - they were living apart by June 1921. That is why every spouse search failed.
next: Search 1921 on his OCCUPATION - 'Joiner' is now recorded on his profile from the 1893 probate
Work backwards from the 1939 Register, where he is already attached in the West Riding, to get an address to search from
Consider that he may simply not be in England in June 1921

blocked.name: Elizabeth Weatherill
tried: 55 Elizabeth Weatherill/Wetherill/Weatherall deaths in the 1870-80 civil index - NONE matches b.~1807 in the Easingwold or York districts
nearest temptations (b.1789 York 1877; b.1793 Sculcoates 1876) are 15+ years out on age - rejected
blocked.found_instead: Bracketed to 1871x1881 by the census itself: James appears in 1881 at Lillings Ambo as head of a household of ONE. Her ABSENCE is the evidence.
next: The Sheriff Hutton / Lillings Ambo parish BURIAL register, not the civil index

blocked.name: Jane Farrar
blocked.found_instead: Her age is irreconcilable between censuses: 28 in 1871 (b.~1843) but 49 in 1881 (b.~1832). An 11-year jump, far past normal census slop. The tree carries abt 1832.
next: Her baptism at Wibsey would decide which age is right, and that decides which death to look for
Do NOT search for a death until the birth year is settled - the wrong year is why nothing matches

blocked.name: James R. Best
tried: 1851 exact birthplace Tadcaster - zero
1851 loosened + Yorkshire residence - 52 results, residence filter did not bind, no Tadcaster-born James Best
1851 EVERY Best resident in Tadcaster - only 12 people, no James and no Thomas Best household at all
1861 same approaches - 46 results, none Tadcaster-born
1861 with surname dropped entirely - 191,347 results; without a surname the birth filter stops binding, so the drop-the-surname trick does NOT work here
blocked.found_instead: He is recorded as ROBERT in 1871 and JAMES R. in 1881 - James ROBERT Best, who went by Robert. Also: BEST indexes as BERT (a whole Matthew Bert household sits among the Tadcaster Bests).
next: The 1868 Bradford Cathedral marriage register IMAGE - it should give his father Thomas Best's occupation and residence
His father Thomas Best I352793764615 is a blank shell with ZERO records; without his household there is no way to recognise James as a child in 1841/1851. Thomas is the real blocker.
Try searching as ROBERT Best, not James - that is the name the 1871 enumerator used

blocked.name: Henry Bausor
tried: 1841 Southwell household indexed as BARRSON (HO107/867 bk 12 f.16 p.13) - Henry 45, Anne 45, John 15, Martha 10, George 2. READ THE IMAGE: the ages really are 15 and 10, not 18 and 20.
Searched every George Bausor baptism in Notts 1813-1922 - only 8 exist, the sole Southwell one is 1861 to JOHN & Anne, so no George to Henry & Ann
blocked.found_instead: REJECTED as a different Henry & Ann Bausor. Our Martha was a MINOR marrying in 1843 and 28 in 1851 (b.~1823); a Martha aged 10 in 1841 would have been twelve at that wedding. Southwell is a Bausor village - there is also a Thomas Bausor household, a Paul Bauser, and John Bausor's family baptising there for twenty years.
next: Ann was at Lambley in 1851 as 'mother-in-law', which reads like a widow - so Henry probably died 1841x1851, but a death in that window would not tell us WHICH Henry
JOHN CAMPION, who witnessed the 1818 marriage with his mark, is the live lead upward - on a bride's side a same-surname witness is nearly always her father or brother

blocked.name: Henry Sturdy Whitehead
tried: East Riding baptisms (coll 62140) for Henry Whitehead b.1814 Holme upon Spalding Moor - 25 results, no match
same collection searching the given name 'Henry Sturdy' - 38 results, no Sturdy anywhere
York marriages (62233) for a Sturdy bride marrying a Whitehead - 396 results, spouse filter did not bind
blocked.found_instead: The 1844 Howden marriage register (coll 62141, rec 105674426, PE121/21) names his father as WILLIAM WHITEHEAD, INNKEEPER, and Henry himself as a GENTLEMAN'S SERVANT of Howden. It does NOT name his mother - an 1837-form register names fathers only - so the STURDY-as-maiden-name theory is untouched. But his father is no longer a blank name.
next: STRONG NEW ANCHOR: hunt a WILLIAM WHITEHEAD, INNKEEPER, at HOWDEN marrying c.1810-14. An innkeeper is licensed, rated and listed in trade directories - he leaves far more paper than a labourer, and that is a much better search than the blind East Riding baptism sweep that already failed.
Alehouse recognizances / victuallers' licences for Howden, and any Howden trade directory of the 1820s-40s, should name the inn and place the family
If the marriage is found it should name the bride - and the test of the whole theory is whether her surname is STURDY

blocked.name: William Whitehead
tried: WHICH INN DID HE KEEP? Not found. The 1844 Howden marriage register gives his rank as 'Innkeeper' and stops there - marriage registers name the trade, never the premises.

1841 census, every WHITEHEAD resident in Howden - only THREE exist (indexed as WHITCHEAD): John abt 1821, Hannah abt 1811, Ellen abt 1840. NO WILLIAM. So he had probably died before 1841, or was living outside Howden.
UK City and County Directories (coll 3145), Whitehead + Howden exact - ZERO. With name+residence unfiltered it returns 2,835 Leeds hits, so the residence filter is not binding in that collection.
NOTE: the 1844 register did NOT annotate him 'deceased' - but many registers omit that, so it is NOT evidence he was alive in 1844.
blocked.found_instead: *** SOLVED: HE KEPT THE 'HARE AND HOUNDS' AT HOLME UPON SPALDING MOOR. *** East Riding Archives CalmView catalogue, Quarter Sessions QDT/2/10 (Alehouse Recognizances, Holme Beacon Division) - FOUR annual recognizances, all the same man: 1822 QDT/2/10/18, 1823 QDT/2/10/61, 1825 QDT/2/10/104, 1826 QDT/2/10/145. Access Open. The 1822 surety is JOHN SMITH of Holme, carrier. THE CLINCHER IS THE PLACE: Henry Sturdy Whitehead was BORN AT HOLME UPON SPALDING MOOR in 1814, so William kept the pub in the very village his son was born in. That is why every Howden search failed - he was never a Howden innkeeper; he was a HOLME innkeeper whose son later moved to Howden (~7 miles). These four dates, 1822-1826, are the FIRST DATES THIS MAN HAS EVER HAD.
next: *** SEARCH HOLME UPON SPALDING MOOR, NOT HOWDEN. *** Everything was looked for in the wrong parish. His marriage c.1810-14 and his burial are most likely at HOLME, and so is HENRY STURDY WHITEHEAD'S BAPTISM (b.1814 Holme) - which is the record that would finally name his MOTHER and settle the 'Sturdy = maiden name' theory. That baptism is now the single most valuable target on this branch.

Check the alehouse recognizances EITHER SIDE of 1822-1826 - the series is annual, so his licence probably starts earlier and ends later. The end date may effectively date his death or his giving up the house. Series is QDT/2/10, Holme Beacon Division, East Riding Archives, and it is catalogued ITEM BY ITEM online and free.
JOHN SMITH of Holme, CARRIER - his 1822 surety. Sureties are usually neighbours, kin or fellow tradesmen; worth a look for a family connection.

Order copies of the four recognizances from East Riding Archives (AccessStatus: Open) - the documents themselves may give more than the catalogue title, and would be a lovely thing to have for a story.

METHOD NOTE FOR THE WHOLE PROJECT: this was found in a FREE PUBLIC LOCAL-AUTHORITY ARCHIVE CATALOGUE (calmview.eastriding.gov.uk) after three Ancestry collections failed. When a trade is known, search the county archive catalogue EARLY - Quarter Sessions papers list tradesmen by name and often name their premises.

blocked.name: William Laverack
blocked.found_instead: Also a zero-record shell until the same 1844 register gave him GAMEKEEPER, 1844. His daughter Martha was baptised 25 Jan 1818 at Snaith (Cowick), and the tree carries a wife, Ann Wright, whose maiden name came off that baptism transcription.
next: A gamekeeper works for a named estate - the Howden/Cowick area landowner's records may place him
William x Ann Wright's own marriage, c.1810s, Snaith/Cowick - it would also cross-check the Wright surname, which currently rests only on a baptism transcription

blocked.name: Thomas LACKEY
tried: 1891 Thomas Lackey b.1863 Birkenhead exact - 19 results, none in Yorkshire
1891 every LACKEY resident in Hunslet - only 8 people, no Thomas
1891 every Lackey born BIRKENHEAD in the whole of England - only FOUR exist, and neither Hunslet one is him
1891 Thomas Lackey across the Leeds district - 123 results, all Lakey/Lockey of the wrong age and birthplace
1891 on the IRISH reading (after the birthplace work below): every Thomas Lackey born IRELAND in the whole census - only FOUR, none in Yorkshire

1891 SURNAME DROPPED ENTIRELY - every Thomas born IRELAND resident in HUNSLET - 44 people, and none of them is him. Checked the one tempting hit, 'Tom Ewes, 30, b. BELFAST, Boarder' (rec 5775315): he boards with Robert Paul's family, no Emms and no Lackey connection, age 30 against Thomas's 27, and 'Ewes' is not a plausible mangling of 'Lackey'. REJECTED.
So BOTH readings of his birthplace have now been searched and BOTH fail. He is not findable in 1891 as a Birkenhead-born or an Ireland-born Thomas Lackey, nor as any Irish-born Thomas in Hunslet.

THE BRIDE'S HOUSEHOLD - also checked, also negative for him. Sarah Jane Emms IS in 1891 (rec 4027244): aged 20, BOARDER with her sister Elizabeth Emms 19, in Charles E Taylor's house at Hunslet, RG12/3665 folio 24 page 41, ED 35. Thomas is not in that household. Note it is the SAME PIECE (RG12/3665) as the Lackey boarding household but a DIFFERENT enumeration district (ED 37, folio 56) - same corner of Hunslet, not the same street.
blocked.found_instead: A cluster of Lackeys boarding together in Hunslet in Mary H Farrar's household (rec 4833266): JAMES Lackey 40 (b.1851 IRELAND), JOHNNY Lackey 27 (b.1864 Birkenhead), WILLIAM Lackey 13 (b.1878 Hunslet). A second household holds William Lackey 35 (b.1855 Birkenhead) and Mary A Lackey 15. Thomas's parents William Lackey and Mary Anne Milligan were BOTH DEAD by 1882, so orphaned siblings boarding together fits.
next: !! HIS BIRTHPLACE IS IN DOUBT, AND IT MAY BE WHY THE 1891 SEARCH FAILED. The 1921 census gives his birth place as BELFAST, ANTRIM, IRELAND. The tree says 27 Jun 1863 BIRKENHEAD. This family IS Irish - his parents are the Irish line, and a James Lackey b.1851 IRELAND boards with the family in 1891. Birkenhead is exactly the port you land at from Belfast. I searched every Lackey born BIRKENHEAD in the whole 1891 census (only four exist, none him) - if he is indexed as Irish-born, that search was aimed at the wrong birthplace. COMPARE THE BIRTHPLACE ACROSS ALL HIS CENSUSES (1871, 1881, 1901, 1911, 1921) BEFORE CHANGING ANYTHING, then re-run 1891 on the winner.

RESOLVED - the 'Johnny Lackey' candidate is his BROTHER. Thomas's profile carries a brother John Lackey b. abt 1864 Birkenhead, so 'Johnny Lackey, 27, b.1864 Birkenhead', the boarder in the 1891 Hunslet household, is John and not Thomas. Do not attach it to Thomas.
He married in OCTOBER 1891 at Hunslet, six months after the census, so in April he was single and probably boarding - check the household his bride Sarah Jane Emms was in
Work out his siblings first; the boarding cluster is probably the family

blocked.name: WILLIAM SAWDON
tried: coll 62230 (York CoE Baptisms/Marriages/Burials 1538-1812) for a William Sawdon baptism c.1745 Weaverthorpe - the date filter does not bind and every INDEXED Weaverthorpe Sawdon baptism it returns starts in the 1780s (1784, 1786, 1787, 1789, 1797, 1811, 1812). The 1730s-50s stretch of that register is barely indexed - normal for pre-1754.

One 1740s entry did surface, almost by accident: Rebekah Sawdon, bapt 2 Nov 1744 Weaverthorpe, father Wm Sawdon (rec 610198, Borthwick PR/WEA/1). So a Wm Sawdon WAS fathering children at Weaverthorpe in the 1740s - but that record is about Rebekah, not about our William, and proves nothing on its own.
blocked.found_instead: FATHER SOLVED - it was in a record ALREADY ATTACHED to him and never opened: William Sawden, bapt 1737 Weaverthorpe, FATHER WILLIAM SAWDEN (Borthwick PR/WEA/1, coll 62230 rec 610044). The parent link had simply never been made. Father now on the tree as a new gen 10. Two things this exposed, BOTH STILL OPEN: (1) his birth is recorded as 1745 but he was BAPTISED 1737 - impossible, and the 1745 is the one resting on Geneanet/tree sources while 1737 comes from the register, so he was probably born c.1737; (2) his profile DISPLAYS the baptism as '8 May 1737 North Burton, York' but the attached register entry says WEAVERTHORPE - the fact carries two sources that disagree on the parish, and the derivative index looks to be the one that is wrong.
next: FIX THE BIRTH YEAR: 1745 cannot stand against a 1737 baptism. A register beats a derivative index. This is a deliberate fact edit on a direct ancestor, so it needs doing on purpose rather than as a side effect.
FIX OR SPLIT THE BAPTISM PLACE: North Burton vs Weaverthorpe. Read the image at coll 62230 rec 610044 and let the register decide.

MOTHER: adding the father surfaced an Ancestry hint 'Francis Tindall - Potential mother', and one of the floating Geneanet imports in the tree is 'Frances Tindale b.1690 Weaverthorpe'. The hint and the junk import AGREE, which proves nothing - they are probably the same strangers' tree feeding both. DO NOT LINK HER. Prove it instead from a William Sawden x [bride] marriage at Weaverthorpe before 1737.

The 7 floating Sawdons baptised Weaverthorpe 1737-1756 are sourced ONLY to Geneanet, not to any register, and the register has Rebekah 1744 where the import has Ann 1744. Do not link them on a name-and-date match - prove them from PR/WEA/1 or leave them.

blocked.name: William Sawden (the elder, gen 10)
tried: coll 62230 for a William Sawden marriage c.1723-1743 - only TWO turned up in the whole collection, and neither is at Weaverthorpe
blocked.found_instead: CANDIDATE, NOT ATTACHED: William Sawden x SARAH BELL, 14 Oct 1732, WHARRAM PERCY (Borthwick PR/WP/1, coll 62230 rec 1371656). Right name, five years before the 1737 baptism, and Wharram Percy is about 4 miles from Weaverthorpe - but it is a DIFFERENT PARISH and nothing ties this William to the Weaverthorpe one. The rival, William Sawdon x Ann, 2 Jun 1752 Appleton le Street, is too late for a son baptised 1737 and is rejected.
next: !! SARAH BELL (from a parish register) CONTRADICTS Ancestry's hint of 'Francis Tindall - potential mother', which merely agrees with a Geneanet import already floating in problems.txt §6 (Frances Tindale b.1690 Weaverthorpe). Two copies of the same unsourced tree are not corroboration. DO NOT LINK TINDALL.

To settle it: find other children baptised to William Sawden at Weaverthorpe in the 1730s-40s where the register names a MOTHER. Note the 1737 baptism names only the father - pre-1813 registers usually do - so the marriage may be the only route to her.

Borthwick PR/WEA/1 (Weaverthorpe) needs reading by eye for the 1720s-40s; the index does not reach it. Rebekah Sawdon bapt 2 Nov 1744 father Wm (rec 610198) is in that register and is probably a daughter of the same man.

blocked.name: Edith Champion
blocked.found_instead: DELIBERATELY LEFT UNLINKED - we do not know, and Chris's call is to leave her that way. Her mother Annie Discombe married TWICE: William Mayne 12 Oct 1884 Leeds (he DIED March 1888), then Charlie Champion 3 Feb 1889 Buslingthorpe. Edith was born abt 1888, which falls in the ELEVEN-MONTH GAP between the two. So she is either William Mayne's posthumous child or Charlie Champion's born before the wedding. Her four siblings are settled - Emily Mayne (b.1887) to Mayne, Willie/Jessie/Walter to Champion - she is the only one that cannot be placed by date.
next: ONLY her birth registration settles it - exact date, and whether a father is named. Do not assign one on the surname: 'recorded as Champion' is a surname on a tree, not evidence, and a birth a few months before a marriage is a pattern this family already supplies once (Arthur Ingleby).

If she was born Sep-Dec 1888 she was conceived before William Mayne's March 1888 death, which would favour Mayne; a birth early in 1888 favours the same. A late-1888 conception would favour Champion. The registration quarter is therefore the whole question.

blocked.name: Emma Ingleby
tried: four-collection sweep for a Thomas Ingleby x Emma marriage - nothing
the Pontefract lead - re-tested and rejected on sound grounds: a Q1-1892 registration is chronologically impossible against Arthur's twice-corroborated 4 Oct 1892 birth
death sweep 1901-1911 - nothing
blocked.found_instead: There is probably no marriage to find - Thomas and Emma may never have married, which is consistent with Arthur's birth registration.
next: Treat the maiden name as the target, not the marriage. Arthur's birth registration or his own 1913 marriage lines may name her.
